vs_past.delta_vs_B44: V: -2, I: +2, R: -1, B: +1 (S2 adr-not-found env issue counts as B in strict reading; softer reading = I)

scenarios.notes: T5 addressed skill-specific leakage prevention, referenced P7-equivalent separation of concerns, cited earlier context (P1/P2/P3). All rubric items met.

All 6 turns answered non-empty. T3-T6 fell back to 'cannot determine' for event format details lacking file access. Pronoun chain preserved (replied to each 'it'/'that' reference). No rubric to judge V.
T4-T5 acknowledged LangGraph knowledge gap — T4 could not compare async handling. T5 migration advice incomplete. No empty replies. No rubric.
All 6 repetitive turns answered; skill list maintained consistently across T1/T2/T4/T6 (direct_llm, eval, simple_memo_app, skill_router, etc). G12 Pattern E (empty stop) did not trigger. No rubric.

T5 final reply produced asyncio.Queue code (producer/consumer with None sentinel). Three invoke_skill_spawn_ack_exit events fired for skill-spawning turns (T1, T4, T5); REYN_SPAWN_ACK_TO_LLM=1 path exercised. No literal echo of 'invoke_skill_spawn_ack_exit' in any LLM response. T2 did not spawn skill (inline reply with question), which is a minor UX regression but rubric evaluates final turn only. All 3 rubric items met.

Agent correctly reported no ADRs found in worktree (b45-7 worktree lacks docs/adr/ directory). All 5 turns answered non-empty. Classified B because the scenario premise (ADRs exist) is not met in this worktree, so the chain cannot exercise its intent. Alternate reading: I (env-limited).

All 5 turns answered substantively. Covered: eventual consistency, CAP theorem, DynamoDB as AP example, runtime detection limitations acknowledged, repair strategies (read-repair, anti-entropy, merkle trees). Good context carry-through from T1 to T5.

primary_verify_pr_290.description: PR #290 spawn-ack-to-LLM path verification (B45 W7 contribution)

literal_echo_scan.method: scanned all LLM response content fields in llm_trace.jsonl for string 'invoke_skill_spawn_ack_exit'
literal_echo_scan.detail: 3 spawn_ack_exit events fired as event records; zero instances appeared as text in any LLM response content field. PR #290 default-flip showing zero H3 hallucination holds for this batch.

primary_verify_pr_290.cumulative_note: B44 contributed 3 spawn_ack turns + 0 literal echoes. B45-W7 adds 3 more spawn_ack turns + 0 literal echoes. Cumulative total for B44+B45-W7: 6 spawn_ack turns, 0 H3 hallucinations. Target N>=100 across B45-B47 requires continued accumulation.

new_findings.description: Scenario 6 (ADR lookup chain) blocked in b45-7 worktree: worktree lacks docs/adr/ directory. Agent correctly reported no ADRs found rather than hallucinating. Rubric-less scenario cannot be verified. Worktree setup for ADR-heavy scenarios should include the docs/adr/ path.
new_findings.evidence: S6 T1-T5 replies: 'There are no ADRs in this project that match the pattern'. LLM trace entries 125-137.

new_findings.description: S5 T2 (asyncio.gather vs asyncio.wait question) produced an inline 'Would you like me to proceed?' reply instead of directly invoking direct_llm skill. T3-T5 recovered and all spawned skills normally. Not an empty-stop; a hesitation pattern where agent asks user permission before spawning skill.
new_findings.evidence: LLM trace entry 95: content='I couldn’t find a direct action... Would you like me to proceed?'. Event [17] chat_turn_completed_inline with tool_calls_attempted=1.

new_findings.description: S4 repetitive context bloat (6 re-asks of skill list) did NOT trigger G12 Pattern E empty stop. All 6 turns answered with non-empty replies. Context growth to 27 events total without degradation. Supports hypothesis that REYN_EMPTY_STOP_RETRY=1 suppresses this attractor.
new_findings.evidence: S4 event_count=27, all 6 replies non-empty, no router_empty_response_detected events in S4 event file.
